_comment: Phase 6g iteration 2 — re-fired with softened criteria 3 and 4. Iteration 1 (webset_01kqwqf5sd) had 3.3% pass rates on criteria 3+4 (the $1.5M threshold and 'tried-and-failed' clause). Cancelled and re-fired. Qualifiers stay in searchQuery (qualitative anchor) and enrichments (still extracted per company). Webset ID: webset_01kqwqs4hpa3a05ats2zzsm9rs. Generated 2026-05-05.
searchQuery: Non-AI-native enterprise companies with growing AI inference workloads at production scale (>$1.5M annual inference spend) whose primary business is something other than AI infrastructure — banks, payment networks, health insurers, life insurers, healthcare delivery and oncology, cybersecurity software, AIOps and observability platforms, retail, automotive (including autonomous vehicles), consumer goods, manufacturing, HR/finance SaaS, transportation. These companies CONSUME inference internally (RAG-based document Q&A, fraud detection, claims adjudication, embeddings, summarization, agentic workflows) but their core business is NOT producing or selling AI inference. They face regulatory or contractual constraints (HIPAA, PCI DSS, SOC 2, GDPR, FedRAMP) that block them from using multi-tenant inference clouds, OR they have explicitly tried Fireworks, Together, Baseten, Modal and been unable to reach production due to data security/residency. Strongest fit: data-sensitive verticals like cybersecurity, AIOps, healthcare insurance and delivery, fintech, life insurance, government-adjacent SaaS, and companies similar to BigPanda, Varonis, UnitedHealth Group, Mastercard, Capital One, ABInBev, Ford, Mobileye. EXCLUDE: AI infrastructure vendors, AI cloud providers, GPU resellers, AI training data companies, ML platform companies, sovereign AI cloud providers, foundation model labs, AI-native startups whose primary product is AI inference or AI products, including but not limited to: Fireworks, Together, Baseten, Modal, Groq, Cerebras, Crusoe, Coreweave, Nebius, Voltage Park, Substrate AI, Inferact, Scale AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Perplexity, Decagon, BentoML, TrueFoundry, Doubleword, Contextual, Poetiq, Impala, Weights & Biases, Comet.

searchCriteria.description: Enterprise company whose primary business is not AI infrastructure, AI inference, or AI-native products (exclude AI infrastructure vendors, AI cloud providers, GPU resellers, ML platform companies, foundation model labs, and AI-native startups; specifically NOT Fireworks, Together, Baseten, Modal, Groq, Cerebras, Crusoe, Coreweave, Nebius, Substrate AI, Inferact, Scale AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Perplexity, Decagon)

Operates in a data-sensitive vertical such as banking, payment networks, health insurance, life insurance, healthcare delivery, oncology, cybersecurity software, AIOps, observability platforms, retail, automotive (including autonomous vehicles), consumer goods, manufacturing, HR/finance SaaS, transportation, or government-adjacent SaaS

Has publicly documented use of production AI/ML inference (e.g., RAG-based search, fraud detection, claims adjudication, embeddings, document processing, summarization, recommendation systems, or agentic workflows) — evidenced by engineering blog posts, conference talks, earnings call mentions, AI/ML platform job postings, or third-party press

Operates under regulatory, contractual, or industry-specific data-handling obligations that affect cloud and AI deployment choices (e.g., HIPAA, PCI DSS, SOC 2, GDPR, FedRAMP, FFIEC, NYDFS, state-level health regulations, customer-mandated data residency, or proprietary/sensitive customer data sensitivity)

enrichments.description: Industry classification, SIC or NAICS preferred

Most recent annual revenue in USD with reporting year and source (10-K, news article, or third-party industry analyst). For private companies, use most recent published estimate or funding-round commentary. Format: '$X.XB (FY2025) | source URL'

Up to 5 currently active job postings relevant to AI inference, ML platform engineering, ML infrastructure, GPU/CUDA engineering, model serving, or SRE-for-AI roles, with title, key technologies/responsibilities mentioned (vLLM, Triton, TensorRT, Kubeflow, KServe, Ray Serve, etc.), and URL to the posting. Source: company careers pages, LinkedIn jobs, third-party job boards. Format: numbered list with title | tech keywords | URL per row.

Recent (last 12 months) public financial and strategic signals: 10-K commentary on AI infrastructure spend or strategy, earnings call quotes from CTO/CIO/CEO about AI cost or capacity, leadership changes (new CTO, Chief AI Officer, Head of ML Platform), M&A activity in AI/data, strategic partnership announcements with cloud providers or inference vendors. Use SEC filings, earnings transcripts, Bloomberg/Reuters/Wall Street Journal as primary sources. Format: 3-5 specific signals with quoted phrasing and source URL inline.

Documented evidence that this company runs production AI inference at scale, citing engineering blogs, conference talks (KubeCon, Ray Summit, NeurIPS industry track, AWS re:Invent customer story, Google Cloud Next), or third-party technical press (NOT the company's own marketing pages). Include: estimated annualized inference spend in USD if derivable (range like '$2M-$5M' is fine), the scale of inference workloads (requests/second, tokens/day, model sizes), the current inference stack (GPU types, framework like vLLM/TensorRT/Triton, deployment pattern), and any documented pain points (cost overruns, latency issues, capacity constraints, GPU stockouts). Format: 2-4 sentence narrative with at least one source URL inline. If the company appears to BE an inference vendor or AI infrastructure provider rather than a consumer, mark this field 'NOT APPLICABLE - vendor not buyer'.

Documented data residency, sovereignty, or regulatory constraints that limit this company's AI deployment choices. Sources: compliance pages on company website, regulatory filings (10-K risk factors), customer security agreements, security whitepapers, third-party news on data breaches or compliance issues. Specifically check for: HIPAA (PHI), PCI DSS (payment data), SOC 2 (customer trust), GDPR (EU data), FedRAMP/IL5 (US government), state-level health regulations. Format: 2-3 sentence narrative followed by 'description | URL'.

THE WOW SIGNAL: Documented evidence that this company has tried any inference cloud (Fireworks, Together, Baseten, Modal, Groq, Cerebras, Anyscale) and been blocked from production deployment due to data security, residency, or compliance constraints. Look for: engineering blog posts about AI vendor evaluations, Reddit/HackerNews discussions from company engineers, conference talks describing failed pilots, third-party analyst notes. If found, include: which provider, what blocker, source URL. If not found explicitly but plausible based on the company's regulatory posture, mark 'plausible - not documented'. If clearly inapplicable (company is AI-native), mark 'N/A - AI-native'.

Top 2 likely buyer/champion personas at this company who would own inference infrastructure decisions. Look for: VP/Head of Platform Engineering, VP/Head of ML Infrastructure, Head of Cloud Infrastructure, VP Site Reliability for AI, Chief AI Officer, Director of AI Platform. EXCLUDE: ML engineers / applied ML / data scientists (these are antagonist personas - they see external inference vendors as a threat to their role). EXCLUDE: Security, compliance, governance teams (founder explicitly does NOT sell to these). For each: full name, exact role, LinkedIn URL. Format: 'Name | Title | LinkedIn URL' per row.

5 verifiable third-party source URLs from credible publications (Bloomberg, Reuters, Wall Street Journal, TechCrunch, The Information, Forrester/Gartner notes, SEC.gov, IEEE/ACM publications, company engineering blogs hosted on engineering.<company>.com or <company>.com/blog) supporting the above enrichments. Avoid the company's own marketing pages, Wikipedia, Reddit, generic LLM comparison sites. Format: numbered list with title | URL.
